page: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8375C processor clocked at 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate solid performance across memory-intensive and computational workloads. The instance achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1892.28 and a multi-core score of 15466.97. Memory bandwidth tests show 828.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 97.52 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 15.88 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 7564.77. For text and compression tasks, the system delivers 28.2 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression and 46.72 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The inclusion of 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside 128 GB of RAM offers high resource density, making this instance class cost-efficient for workloads requiring fast local scratch space and large memory footprints without relying solely on network-attached storage. The hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive applications, high-performance databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with cached read bandwidth reaching 828.39 GB/sec and Redis SET operations performing at 15.88 million operations per second. The instance also demonstrates capable processing power, scoring 21005.41 on the Passmark CPU test and 9796.0 on Geekbench multi-core. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local NVMe storage, the r6id.4xlarge is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, designed for high-performance database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, 128 GB RAM, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 8.0 GB per core allocation
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and data processing
